assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a recognised intragroup monetary item denominated in a foreign currency and gives rise to foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
The question is assessed in the consolidated financial statements of the group.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the foreign currency risk component
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary item that creates foreign exchange effects in consolidated profit or loss can be designated in a fair value hedge for only its foreign currency risk component.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of the foreign currency risk component
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also permits a recognised asset or liability to be hedged for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, so the eligible intragroup FX component is a genuine alternative hedge route.
